skill.name: reactbits
skill.description: Search, browse, and install animated React components from ReactBits.dev (135+ components). Use when the user wants to add UI effects, animations, backgrounds, or interactive components to their React/Next.js project.
skill.summary: Browse and install 135+ animated React components from ReactBits.dev for UI effects, animations, and interactive elements.
skill.author: hwj23016021425

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 26 potential issues, but all are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections triggered on MCP tool name references (e.g., `mcp__reactbits__list_components`) in documentation, not actual shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections triggered on documentation text, not cryptographic code. The SKILL.md file is a benign skill description that documents MCP tool usage for browsing and installing React components. No malicious intent or unsafe code patterns were found.

low_findings.title: MCP tool references detected as external commands
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged MCP tool name patterns like `mcp__reactbits__list_components` as potential shell command execution. These are documentation references to MCP server tools, not actual command invocations. The skill operates through Claude's MCP protocol.

content.user_title: Add Animated React Components from ReactBits
content.value_statement: Building polished React UIs with animations takes hours of setup. This skill lets Claude search, browse, and install 135+ pre-built animated components from ReactBits.dev in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Search 135+ animated React components by name or keyword from ReactBits.dev
List components by category: Animations, Backgrounds, Text Animations, Components, Buttons, Forms, Loaders
Retrieve full source code for any component with Tailwind or CSS style variants
Get usage examples and demo code for proper component integration
Guide installation of required dependencies like framer-motion, GSAP, and Three.js
limitations: Components are sourced from ReactBits.dev only — no custom or private component sources
Some advanced components require manual dependency installation by the user
Style variants are limited to Tailwind, CSS, and default options

use_cases.title: Build a landing page with animations
use_cases.description: Ask Claude to find and add animated backgrounds, text effects, and interactive components to a new landing page.

use_cases.title: Add a specific UI effect to an existing project
use_cases.description: Request a particular animation like a blob cursor, particle effect, or text shimmer and have it integrated with proper imports.

use_cases.title: Explore available components before choosing
use_cases.description: Browse the full ReactBits catalog by category to compare options before committing to an implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Find an animated component
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ReactBits for a [component type] animation effect. Show me 3 options with brief descriptions.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to discover available animation options

prompt_templates.title: Get component source code
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the full source for the [component name] component from ReactBits using Tailwind style. Also show me the demo code so I understand how to use it.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to add a specific component to their project

prompt_templates.title: Build a full page
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to build a personal portfolio website with a dark, tech-inspired style. Use purple as the main color. Recommend 4-5 ReactBits components that would work well together.
prompt_templates.scenario: Website builder mode — user provides high-level intent

prompt_templates.title: Integration with dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Add the [component name] to my project. Check what dependencies it needs, install missing ones, and create the component file in components/ folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants end-to-end integration with dependency handling

output_examples.output:
Found 4 matching components:

1. **BlobCursor** — A gooey blob that follows your cursor with smooth fluid motion
2. **ClickSpark** — Click anywhere to create a burst of spark particles
3. **Crosshair** — A custom crosshair cursor that replaces the default pointer
4. **SplashCursor** — Colorful splash effects trail behind mouse movement

Which one would you like to add to your project?

Retrieved BlurText component (Text Animations category):

**Style:** Tailwind
**Dependencies:** framer-motion
**Has CSS:** No

The component animates text by blurring it in from focus, letter by letter or word by word. It accepts props for animation duration, delay between letters, and trigger type (in-view or on-mount).

Would you like me to add this to your project? I can install framer-motion and create the component file.

best_practices: Always check component dependencies before integrating — many effects require framer-motion, GSAP, or Three.js
Ask the user to choose between Tailwind and CSS styles to match their project setup
Use the Website Builder Mode flow when the user expresses broad intent like 'build me a site'
anti_patterns: Do not install all 135 components at once — select only what the user actually needs
Do not skip the style preference question — Tailwind vs CSS affects the entire integration
Do not assume dependencies are already installed — always verify before importing

faq.question: What is ReactBits.dev?
faq.answer: ReactBits.dev is a library of 135+ animated, interactive React components including backgrounds, text effects, buttons, loaders, and more.

faq.question: Do these components work with Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes. ReactBits components are standard React components and work in any React framework including Next.js, Vite, and Create React App.

faq.question: What dependencies might I need?
faq.answer: Common dependencies include framer-motion for animations, GSAP for timeline effects, and Three.js for 3D backgrounds. The skill will tell you what each component requires.

faq.question: Can I use these components commercially?
faq.answer: Most ReactBits components are free to use. Check the ReactBits.dev license page for specific component licensing details before commercial use.

faq.question: How do I customize the colors and styles?
faq.answer: If you choose the Tailwind variant, you can modify Tailwind classes directly. For CSS variants, edit the accompanying CSS file. The skill will create both files for you.

faq.question: Is there a performance cost to using these animations?
faq.answer: Some effects like particle systems and 3D backgrounds are GPU-intensive. The skill will note heavy components so you can make informed decisions about where to use them.
